pathways.title: First Words
pathways.subtitle: How the path begins
pathways.blurb: Eight discourses, in roughly the order they entered the world. Begin here if you have never read the suttas before.

steps.note: The First Sermon. The Four Noble Truths, given at Deer Park.
The Second Sermon. Why none of the five aggregates is ‘mine’.
The charter of free inquiry: do not believe by tradition or report; test for yourself.
The Mettā Sutta. The standard for loving-kindness.
Foundations of Mindfulness — the body, feeling, mind, and dhamma.
The Pairs. Mind precedes everything; if you speak with a corrupted mind, suffering follows.
The raft simile: even right view is a vehicle, not a destination.
The Buddha’s last weeks. ‘Be islands unto yourselves.’ A long read.

pathways.title: For Lay Practitioners
pathways.subtitle: Living the path in the world
pathways.blurb: What the Buddha taught those who were not monastics — about livelihood, family, and giving.

steps.note: Sigālovāda — advice to a young householder. The classic lay code.
The five proper uses of wealth.
Joy, ease, and the gladness of generosity.
The four kinds of happiness available to a householder.
How a layperson should approach work, friendship, and faith.

pathways.title: Three Jewels of the Great Vehicle
pathways.subtitle: Heart, Diamond, Lotus
pathways.blurb: The three sūtras that, more than any others, define the Mahāyāna imagination.

steps.note: Begin with the shortest. Memorize the central line if nothing else.
Diamond Sūtra · The Real Teaching of the Great Way. The bodhisattva’s vow stated paradoxically.
Diamond Sūtra · ‘Give rise to a mind that abides nowhere.’
The closing verse. Memorize this one too.
Lotus · Skillful Means. Why the Buddha teaches different things to different people.
Lotus · The Universal Gateway. The compassion of Avalokiteśvara, who hears the cries of the world.

pathways.title: Walking the Bodhisattva Path
pathways.subtitle: From vow to wisdom
pathways.blurb: Śāntideva’s six chapters in a sequence: how the awakening mind arises, takes hold, and flowers.

steps.note: On the rare arising of bodhicitta, the awakening mind.
Embracing bodhicitta — ‘may I be a bridge, a boat, a raft.’
On patience. ‘There is no evil like hatred.’
Exchanging self and other — the heart of the practice.
On wisdom. The mind that finds nothing to grasp comes to rest.
The closing dedication. ‘As long as space remains…’

pathways.title: Mind Training
pathways.subtitle: Lojong from Atiśa to Tokmé Zangpo
pathways.blurb: How the Tibetan tradition trains the heart to take adversity as the path. Begin with the slogans, then the practices.

steps.note: Train in the preliminaries. Without these, no mind-training takes root.
Tonglen — taking and sending on the breath.
Turn all mishaps into the path.
Be grateful to everyone.
Always maintain only a joyful mind.
37 Practices · The precious human birth — why this life matters.
37 Practices · Bodhicitta — why others’ suffering is your own.
37 Practices · Exchanging self and other.
37 Practices · Mind. Whatever appears is your own awareness.
37 Practices · The closing dedication.

pathways.title: Three Principal Aspects
pathways.subtitle: Tsongkhapa's compass
pathways.blurb: Renunciation, bodhicitta, right view — the three threads Tsongkhapa wove the entire path from.

steps.note: Renunciation. Turning the mind from saṃsāra.
Bodhicitta. Without it, even renunciation is incomplete.
Right view. Emptiness — the wisdom that cuts the root.
Atīśa’s map of three kinds of practitioners.
Padmasambhava — the Buddha is in this present awareness.
Resting in the natural state. The Great Perfection in seven lines.

pathways.title: Transmission of Mind
pathways.subtitle: Bodhidharma to Hakuin
pathways.blurb: Zen reading from the West (Bodhidharma) to Japan (Dōgen, Hakuin) — eight stops on the lineage road.

steps.note: Bodhidharma — to stop seeking is the Way.
Bodhidharma — what practicing the Dharma actually means.
Huineng’s verse. The mirror has no stand.
Huineng on meditation and wisdom as one substance.
The Great Way is not difficult, for those without preferences.
Dōgen — to study the self is to forget the self.
Dōgen — the moon does not get wet, the water is not broken.
Hakuin — all beings are by nature Buddha, as ice is water.

pathways.title: First Koans
pathways.subtitle: Eight cases to sit with
pathways.blurb: Don’t try to solve them. Read each one slowly, then sit. Return to the same case for days.

steps.note: Joshu’s Mu. Begin here.
Joshu washes the bowl. The whole teaching in nine words.
Ordinary mind is the Way.
The Buddha holds up a flower; only Mahākāśyapa smiles.
Three pounds of flax.
Not the wind, not the flag — mind is moving.
The cypress tree in the garden.
Where there is no cold or heat — when cold, cold kills you.

pathways.title: Practice Without Lineage
pathways.subtitle: A starter sequence
pathways.blurb: Eight short pieces, in the order most people benefit from. Read one a day; do the practice afterward.

steps.note: The whole practice: noticing, returning. Begin here.
Body scan — being where the body actually is.
Loving-kindness without the lineage. The wish is the practice.
Working with anxiety — name it, ground, breathe out longer.
Working with anger — anger is information, not instruction.
Setting an intention, before the day starts using you.
End-of-day reflection. Three minutes.
The shortest practice in the book: is anything missing right now?
